College of Witches.name: 대학 - Witches
College of Witches.description:
Also called witches, bards of this college perform for an audience that offers power in return for their talents. They look into the gray, negative space that dwells behind everything in the material world. The Netherworld is their stage. By inviting the ghosts who watch them perform to follow along beside them, a witch can call on them for aid.

Witches are peculiar to most folks in the Beast World, but their work isn’t necessarily offensive. A ghost’s bid for attention is always troublesome, and occasionally dangerous. Anyone who knows of the world a witch plays in is grateful that someone else is the subject of their fawning.

Mercy Domain.name: 자비 권역 - Mercy Domain
Mercy Domain.description:
When gods of mercy bestow their gifts on mortals, the most gentle and kind-hearted of their followers are chosen to wield their power in a unique way. The domain of mercy is divine might used to a different end than most others. Rather than a tool of destruction, this domain is a potent means to help others find redemption. They seek the good in all creatures, and rebuke dealers of death as a last resort. Clerics with this power hold open the door of forgiveness to help lost souls find the path to repentance. Good-aligned deities maintain this divine portfolio, particularly ones who shun violence and focus on forgiveness and strengthening one’s will to resist wrath. In the Beast World, almost all clerics of the Mercy domain are worshippers of the Beast Mother Pirhoua.

Oath of Revolution.name: 맹세 - Revolution
Oath of Revolution.description:
The Oath of Revolution is to aid the common people in seizing their own power. Paladins who take it keep their eyes open and focused, seeking the corruption of the influential. When they find oppression, they are empowered to make the call to collective action.

In the Beast World, some paladins of the justice goddess Dramphine take the Oath of Revolution to become the watchdogs of the powerful. From the noble courts of Allemance to the Oric batkos’ halls, Revolution paladins inspire those downtrodden by rotted social structures to take hold of their own destinies.

Tenets of Revolution

Paladins who take the Oath of Revolution recite these three words as a guiding light pointing themtoward righteous action.

Judgment. The weak and powerless trust in your discernment, thus you owe them a verdict guided by righteousness. You are tasked with soberly recognizing the world’s hidden corruption.

Conviction. When you find people treated unfairly, your immediate priority is inspiring them to action. Whenever possible, you must aid the oppressed in establishing a better path for themselves. You are insurrection’s shepherd, not its sword.

Equity. Strive to be the archetype of fairness and demonstrate the worthiness of law. Leaders look to you for insight into how a community should mete out justice. Any decay in their understanding of this is your responsibility. oath spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed in the Oath of Revolution Spells table. See the Sacred Oath class feature for how oath spells work.

The Ghost God.name: 유령신 - The Ghost God
The Ghost God.description:
The Ghost God

The human gods are dead. Their remaining divinity spilled together and mixed, now a single consciousness that calls out from the Broken World. This is the Ghost God, a patron voice from a decaying world, clinging to existence through its warlocks.

From a young age, the beasts and brethren destined to become the Ghost God’s warlocks are wracked with migraines, mood swings, and nightmares of otherworldly whispers. Their future patron is calling out, but the voice is muffled and distorted by the veil between worlds. With the guidance of other warlocks, an adolescent with these symptoms can embrace their power and answer the call. Those who reject it must learn to tune out the cosmic noise.

The Ghost God’s cause is protecting the humans they watched over in life, with devotion for their people that endures beyond death. The patron offers a precious gift in exchange: power over space itself. They impart some of their unraveling world, eventually allowing their servant to become a patron in their own right

Colors of the Veil

Early in life, the Ghost God’s chosen show physical signs of the dead pantheon’s calling. The most common sign is fur with an unnatural pattern or stark colors. A jagged line slashes the middle of a sloth warlock’s face, dividing natural color and hot pink. Another fox’s fur sports a perfect houndstooth pattern.

Expanded Spell List

The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

The Main Event.name: 메인 이벤트 - The Main Event
The Main Event.description:
The most diverse class of adventurer in the Beast World, fighters are known for a broad mastery of martial combat scenarios. This versatility is unneeded by soldiers and others who wield a weapon as their trade. The guards of a Beylik spice caravan are trained to walk a desert road and deter robberies. To be a fighter in the proper sense requires one to be just as effective fighting in single combat as when facing off against a tentacled creature fifty times their size.

Fighters develop with a mentor more often than any other class. Even a 15th-level fighter makes a regular journey to meet with their final master. Collaboration and competition among fighters sharpens all of their skills. Intelligent fighters study martial arts from respected texts holding the wisdom of generals from the Mantle War and those before. Fighters less inclined to library study still develop with feedback from other pugilists. In the end, a jerk with a grudge can win a fist fight, but fighters are the world’s combat nerds.

Almost every fighter old enough fought in the Invader War. Many got their start as veterans looking to sate a newfound appetite for tactical danger. Luckily for them, opportunities came almost immediately as Dungeon entrances appeared for the first time.

The cultural split between the Bat’yan and Beylik is clearest among fighters. The sneak-and-strike tactics of dense rainforest combat are far removed from the mounted field battle of desert caravanserai. A wickedly effective fighter is birthed by their joining by the Causeway, able to ride an enemy down or fly across treetops to strike unseen.

Martial Archetype: The Main Event

The Storied Histories League is a world of theatrical combat, and its combatants have a need as immediate as the one for food and water: to feed on the energy of the crowd. They absorb and wield the energy that radiates from a captivated audience. The Main Event is the picture of bravado and self-assurance, in the ring and in real fight to the death. They use the excitement of an unseen crowd to attain unrivaled physical perfection. Some combatants take time away from the League to become delvers of the Dungeon. These are The Main Event fighters.
